abstract: Global labor markets are currently undergoing profound transformations driven by socio-political instability, rising structural inequality, worsening employment insecurity, and deteriorating working conditions. In response to these intensifying challenges, international institutions have issued various policy frameworks designed to safeguard and promote the health and well-being of the global workforce. This article examines the strategic integration of the International Labour Organization’s Decent Work Agenda within the broader policy frameworks of the World Health Organization and the World Bank, illustrating how these collaborative agendas shape contemporary labor standards.
